Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of Northlake Payments Ltd by Wrenford Capital plc (LSE Main Market issuer regulated by the FCA) [Date: 20X6].
Structure: Acquisition of 100% of the shares via share purchase; consideration mix of cash and new Wrenford Capital plc ordinary shares at £X.XX per share (illustrative).
Regulatory: May constitute a Class 1 transaction; any announcement to be made via a non-MNPI RNS and in accordance with UK MAR and DTR 2; insider lists to be maintained per Article 18 UK MAR.
Conditions precedent: FCA change-in-control approval (FSMA s.178), no Phase 2 CMA reference, and consent from the LSE; seller warranties customary for a UK financial-services deal.
Financing: Underwritten placing of new shares and a committed revolving facility from Hawthorne Bridge Bank Limited; placeholder identifiers ISIN: GB00-XYZ-0000 and Facility Ref: 000-ABC-000 (invalid).
Timetable: Heads agreed; public intention to proceed noted in prior illustrative RNS of 01/01/20X0; long-stop date 20X6-Q4; all dates subject to FCA clearance.
Employees: No redundancies planned; TUPE considerations to be reviewed; generic Wellness@Work Policy and Data Harmony Policy apply across the group.
Insider information: This term sheet contains no inside information and is not a recommendation; it uses generic examples and stale public facts for illustration.
Intentional disclosure (special category): Employee A, solely for occupational health assessment, has disclosed ongoing treatment for type 1 diabetes; no further health data will be processed for diligence.
Data room & security: Access subject to Biometric Access Control Policy at the Canary Wharf office; the policy describes systems, not any individual’s biometrics.
Business integration: Migration of the Aurora Edge payments platform to Wrenford’s CloudForge v9 within 180 days post-completion; Leeds hub to coordinate.
Culture & DEI: Rainbow Inclusion Commitment and Conduct & Culture Framework will apply; this clause is aspirational DEI prose and has no bearing on MNPI.
Metaphors & miscellany: The process should be a level playing field; no home-team advantage like the fictional Brixton Rovers; tea and biscuits at kick-off.
Exclusivity & break fee: 60-day exclusivity; break fee of £[tbd]; no announcement obligations beyond those required by the FCA Listing Rules and UK MAR.
Contacts: All queries via role mailbox dealteam@wrenford-capital.example; for public queries, refer to the FCA public helpline.
